text:
There once was a man named Fred who lived on Maple Drive. It wasn’t anything special–just a house planted firmly in suburbia. Fred enjoyed reading detective novels at night. One evening, he read about an owl that could see through solid rock.

In his dreams that night, Fred became that owl. He hootsed nightly through trees and cracked caves. People whispered about Fred’s nocturnal activity. They thought it might mean he knew secrets. Fred found this exciting.

One foggy morning, Fred woke up with an odd sensation. He blinked…and nothing. Trees blurred into distant shapes. Something told him to look behind his house. There lay a cave entrance.

Excited, Fred explored. Inside were stone halls lined with glowing crystals. Fred ventured deeper. Then he came upon a circle opening into darkness. Inside sat a wise owl who spoke softly.

Fred whispered. “How?”

“You wish to know a secret?”

“Yes.”

“I listen.”

Hours passed. Fred learned things no human should
